notes.en: Highest sugar content among SL banana cultivars measured (36.4 g/100g). Famous for the distinctive slightly-sour sweet balance.

traits.quote: Ambul cultivar recorded the highest sugar content (36.4 ± 1.69 g/100 g)

group.quote: Sri Lankan banana varieties can be divided into three main groups: the mysore, the kolikuttu, and the Cavendish. Examples of banana varieties coming within the mysore group are the ambul and seeni bananas

traits.group: Cavendish group (with embon, bin kehel, nethrapalam)

traits.quote: Anamalu is used to flavour cakes, breads, desserts and smoothies. anamalu, embon, bin kehel, and nethrapalam come within the Cavendish group
strong resistance to blast and gall midge, plus resistance to brown planthopper. However, it shows Moderately Susceptible response to bacterial leaf blight. Pedigree: Bg 2225-1/Bg 96-3298
moderately resistant to blast and bacterial leaf blight, while demonstrating stronger resistance to gall midge and brown planthopper

release_year.quote: Four rice varieties - Ld 376, BG 377, At 378, and BG 381 - have been introduced as high yielding rice varieties.

expected_yield_kg_per_acre.quote: Can be cultivated in both dry and wet zones, producing 5.3 metric tons of paddy harvest per hectare in the wet zone and 7.3 metric tons in the dry zone

expected_yield_kg_per_acre.review_notes: 5.0 t/ha → 2023 kg/acre. Point estimate stored as both min and max.

traits.quote: Moderately Resistant status against blast and bacterial leaf blight; Resistant traits against gall midge and Moderately Resistant against brown planthopper. Parentage: Bg 367-7//IR 841/Bg 276-5
Susceptible to blast but Moderately Resistant to bacterial leaf blight and brown planthopper, while demonstrating full Resistant status against gall midge
Moderately Resistant to blast, bacterial leaf blight, and brown planthopper. Pedigree : Bg 380/Bg 367-4
Susceptible to blast; Moderately Resistant to bacterial leaf blight; Resistant to gall midge; Moderately Resistant to brown planthopper. 1000 grain weight 13.6 g
moderate resistance to three major threats: Blast, Bacterial Leaf Blight, and Gall Midge

release_year.quote: Four rice varieties - Ld 376, BG 377, At 378, and BG 381 - have been introduced as high yielding rice varieties.
release_year.review_notes: Release year 2023 from Ministry of Agriculture announcement covering this set of four varieties.

expected_yield_kg_per_acre.quote: Can easily be grown in dry zone and produces 6.5 metric tons of paddy harvest per hectare

notes.en: High yielding variety suited to rice-based products.

release_year.quote: Four rice varieties - Ld 376, BG 377, At 378, and BG 381 - have been introduced as high yielding rice varieties.

expected_yield_kg_per_acre.quote: high yielding variety that can successfully be used to produce rice-based products and gives 6.5 metric tons of paddy harvest per hectare

traits.quote: susceptibility to blast; moderate resistance/susceptibility to bacterial leaf blight and brown planthopper; resistant to gall midge
Moderately Resistant to blast; Moderately Susceptible to bacterial leaf blight; Susceptible to both gall midge and brown planthopper. Head rice recovery 63.80%. Pedigree: IR 262/Ld 66

notes.en: Commercial F1 hybrid in DOA's HORDI brinjal recommendation list. 50% flowering at 34 days after planting; yield 45–50 t/ha; ~4,960 g per plant.

expected_yield_kg_per_acre.quote: One variety shows a yield of 45-50 t/ha with a yield per plant of 4960g and 50% flowering at 34 days, while another variety shows 55-60 t/ha with 5600g per plant and 50% flowering at 35 days
expected_yield_kg_per_acre.review_notes: 45–60 t/ha across two hybrids → 18211–24281 kg/acre. Attribution to Amanda F1 / Anjalee F1 inferred; agronomist confirmation recommended.

notes.en: Dark-purple fruited traditional brinjal cultivar maintained by HORDI. Wet-zone yield 15–18 t/ha; up to 20–25 t/ha in better-suited zones.

expected_yield_kg_per_acre.quote: Another variety with dark purple fruits has a potential yield of 15-18 t/ha for the wet zone and 20-25 t/ha
expected_yield_kg_per_acre.review_notes: 15–25 t/ha → 6070–10117 kg/acre. Attribution to Thinnaweli Purple inferred from DOA brinjal page which lists it among recommended dark-purple varieties; agronomist confirmation recommended.

traits.recommendation: Wet Zone; tolerance to iron toxicity

traits.quote: Red pericarp colour and intermediate bold grain shape. Resistant to gall midge, Moderately Resistant to blast; Moderately Resistant / Moderately Susceptible to bacterial leaf blight and brown planthopper. Recommendation: WZ, Tolerance to iron toxicity. Pedigree: Bg 359/Bw 267-3 (short mutant)

notes.en: Husk turns black (kalu) when ripe — hence the name. Medium-sized red grains, high nutritional value, widely used in Ayurveda.

duration_days.quote: The cultivation period of this variety is 3 ½ months.

traits.quote: This variety of rice has red, medium-sized grains, and the name kalu heenati is given to it because the outer husk of the grains turn black (kalu) when they ripen.

traits.nut_yield: Over 100 nuts per palm per year in phenotypes Ran Thembili and Gon Thembili

notes.en: Native Sri Lankan variety; the ideal beverage coconut. Research programme at CRI includes tissue-culture propagation and seasonality mitigation. Gon Thembili also noted for Weligama Leaf Wilt phytoplasma resistance.

traits.quote: King coconut (Cocos nucifera var aurantiaca) is a variety of coconut, native to Sri Lanka, where it is known as Thæmbili. Sri Lanka has one such variety called Aurantiaca, which means orange coloured in Latin, and within this variety, there are four types of coconut, including king coconut – Nawasi Thambili, Rathran Thambili, and Bothal Thambili. The annual average number of bunches varied from 14.9 to 16.8, which is significantly higher than the generally accepted 12–14 bunches in tall coconuts

traits.group: Kolikuttu group (cultivars: kolikuttu, suwendel, puwalu, rath kehel)
traits.flesh_color: highest phenolic content (55.1 mg/100g), highest total antioxidant (948%)
traits.commercial_challenges: Fruits drop from bunches when ripe; thin skin rips — difficult to transport commercially

traits.quote: Kolikuttu bananas are a small to medium-sized varietal, averaging 10 to 15 centimeters in length, and have a short, thick, and plump appearance with a straight to slightly curved, cylindrical shape. the peel is smooth, taut, and thin, splitting open easily when ripe. Despite their popularity, Kolikuttu bananas are challenging to produce commercially as the fruits often drop from the bunches when ripe. The thin skin also rips open, making transport difficult.

release_year.quote: Four rice varieties - Ld 376, BG 377, At 378, and BG 381 - have been introduced as high yielding rice varieties.

expected_yield_kg_per_acre.quote: Can produce 4-6 metric tons of paddy harvest per hectare from paddy cultivation in both dry and wet zones

notes.en: Traditional Sri Lankan rice, ~4 months from seeding to harvest.

duration_days.quote: This crop can be harvested within 4 months from seeding.

traits.cultural_significance: Considered a divine rice in traditional Sinhalese culture

notes.en: Heirloom red rice with high cultural and nutritional value; 3.5-month maturity.

duration_days.quote: Pachchaperumal has long been considered a divine rice in traditional Sinhalese culture. It is a highly nutritious red rice variety that can be harvested within 3 ½ months.

traits.traditional_use: diabetes and oxidative stress management in traditional medicine

notes.en: Adapted to both Dry and Wet zones; high-protein high-zinc red rice used in Ayurveda for diabetes support.

traits.quote: Rath suwandel is a crop cultivated in both the dry and wet zones. It has red, medium-sized grains which are high in protein and zinc, and in traditional medicine, it is used for the treatment of diabetes and oxidative stress.
Seeni bananas are similar to ambul in that they too are small in size. However, they are rounder in shape, and taste distinctly sweeter. The flesh of a seeni banana is sticky-sweet.

notes.en: White-grain small-grain heirloom; ~4 months usually, longer in Yala season.

duration_days.quote: The usual timeline for harvesting this heirloom variety is 4 months but it usually takes longer in the Yala season.

notes.en: Aromatic heirloom variety; organically cultivated with traditional rain-fed methods.

duration_days.quote: Suwandel is an heirloom rice variety, cultivated organically with traditional rain-fed methods in the southern lowlands of Sri Lanka. It is usually 3 months before harvest.

traits.quote: Suwandel is an heirloom rice variety, cultivated organically with traditional rain-fed methods in the southern lowlands of Sri Lanka

notes.en: Early-harvest variety — harvest in 2 months from transplanting. Fruit weight ~85 g, yield ~3.5 kg/plant. Bacterial-wilt resistant.

traits.quote: Thilina is a variety where one fruit weighs around 85g, harvests in 2 months, and from one plant you can get a harvest of 3 1/2 kg. It is also resistant to Bacteria Wilt.

name: TRI 2000 series (TRI 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026)
released_by: Tea Research Institute of Sri Lanka (TRI)

traits.cultivar_set: TRISL 2023, TRISL 2024, TRISL 2025, TRI 2026 (and pigmented TRI 2043)
traits.adoption: Most popular set, covers over 55% of national tea extent
traits.disease_note: TRI 2024 is susceptible to blister blight (reduced net photosynthesis, transpiration, stomatal conductance, WUE under infection)

notes.en: The workhorse clone set of Sri Lankan tea replanting programmes; newer TRI 5000 series supersedes on blister-blight resistance for up-country.

traits.quote: Among Sri Lankan tea clones, TRISL 2023, TRISL 2024, TRISL 2025, and TRI 2026 are most popular and are recommended for replanting. Sri Lankan cultivar TRI 2024 has been identified as susceptible to blister blight disease

name: TRI 5000 series (TRI 5001–5004)
released_by: Tea Research Institute of Sri Lanka (TRI)

traits.quality: TRI 5001 and TRI 5002 produce high-quality black tea; TRI 5003 moderate quality

notes.en: Latest up-country clones: all four resistant to blister blight (the most severe up-country leaf disease).

traits.quote: New tea cultivars recorded around 10-15% yield increase compared to existing TRI 2000, TRI 3000 and TRI 4000 series cultivars. All four tea cultivars are resistant to Blister Blight disease, the most severe leaf disease in the up-country. TRI 5001 and TRI 5002 cultivars produce high quality black tea and TRI 5003 cultivar produces moderate quality black tea
